title: Evidence Search Pattern Selector
description: Apply (Bloom L3) crime-scene search-pattern rules by selecting a search pattern for a given scene, running an animated sweep, and judging coverage, time, and personnel trade-offs against a live appropriateness rating.
creator: Forensic Science Intelligent Textbook
subject: Crime Scene Investigation
Evidence Search Patterns
Scene Documentation
bloomLevel: Apply (L3) — select the appropriate search pattern for a given scene description.
chapter_title: Crime Scene Investigation and Evidence Collection

educational.topic: Crime-Scene Search Patterns and Systematic Coverage
learningObjectives: Select an appropriate search pattern (grid/line, spiral, or zone) for a given scene type and size.
Compare search patterns on the trade-offs of coverage, time, and personnel required.
Explain why a systematic pattern reduces the chance of missed or contaminated evidence compared with a random search.
educational.prerequisites: Concept of a secured crime scene; idea that physical evidence can be small and easily missed; basic grid/coordinate sense

pedagogical.interactionPattern: Select-and-run tool: the learner chooses a pattern, scene type, team size, and speed, runs an animated overhead sweep that colors cells gray (unsearched), yellow (active), and green (searched), and reads a live appropriateness rating plus an end-of-run efficiency summary
pedagogical.assessment: A live fit rating judges the chosen pattern against the chosen scene type; a completion summary reports cells searched, steps elapsed, and an efficiency rating, and a warning appears when more searchers are chosen than a pattern uses efficiently
pedagogical.modelType: Agent-based coverage simulation over a top-down grid with per-pattern path generation
controls: Pattern dropdown (Grid / Line, Spiral Inward, Spiral Outward, Zone / Quadrant)
Scene type dropdown (indoor room, outdoor field, large outdoor area)
